title: The Agent files a support note for the Acme Cloud Q1 AR-aging cleanup in the Records Vault/workpapers under AICPA_SQMS_7Y, with a title or description related to Acme Cloud Q1 AR-aging collectibility or Daniel sign-off support (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly asks for a short support note to be filed with the workpapers. The retention context keeps the note in the correct Brookfield workpaper lane without requiring a separate BlackLine attachment.
evidence: Verify a successful Records Vault/workpaper filing for Acme Cloud with AICPA_SQMS_7Y retention and a title or description matching the AR-aging cleanup/sign-off support purpose.

title: The Agent states in the support note that Acme Cloud AR should be anchored to account 110000 (Accounts Receivable - Trade) and that the old 120000 label in the Q1 aging trail should not be treated as the correct AR control account.
justification: This is the central source-record correction. Runs that accept 120000 as Acme AR miss the chart-of-accounts trap and can produce a misleading client-ready view.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for account 110000, the Accounts Receivable - Trade meaning, and the correction or caveat around the old 120000 aging label.

title: The Agent includes in the support note the Q1 aging position as approximately $4.2M total AR, $2.95M current, $760K in 1-30 days, $310K in 31-60 days, $143K in 61-90 days, and $42K in 90+ days.
justification: The prompt asks to rebuild the aging position, not merely state that aging exists. These bucket values are the source-record aging position the agent must carry into the workpaper support.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for the total AR figure and each bucket amount, accepting clear rounding around the stated values.

title: The Agent states in the support note that CUST-acme-1187 has approximately $82K in 61-90 days and is a contract-consistent net-90 payer requiring only a soft chase or low-concern follow-up.
justification: This customer is the low-risk past-60-day comparator and proves the agent checked receipts rather than treating all old balances alike.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for CUST-acme-1187, the approximate $82K amount, the 61-90 bucket, and the net-90/soft-chase conclusion.

title: The Agent states in the support note that CUST-acme-1294 has approximately $61K in 61-90 days, is standard net-30, has no 2026-Q1 payment activity, and represents real slippage needing active collections follow-up.
justification: This customer is the real-slippage finding. Missing it causes Daniel's sign-off package to understate the collection risk.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for CUST-acme-1294, the approximate $61K amount, the 61-90 bucket, no Q1 payment activity, and the real-slippage/collections conclusion.

title: The Agent states in the support note that CUST-acme-1356 has approximately $42K in 90+ days, has had no payment activity since 2025-Q4, and has an unresolved October 2025 payment-plan request.
justification: This is the highest-risk past-60-day customer and the key collectibility concern.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for CUST-acme-1356, the approximate $42K amount, the 90+ bucket, no receipts since 2025-Q4, and the unresolved October 2025 payment-plan request.

title: The Agent states in the support note that CUST-acme-1356 may warrant an allowance or bad-debt discussion only if Acme CFO direction is obtained, and that Brookfield should not book or push an allowance in the current cycle absent that CFO direction.
justification: The prompt prohibits allowance postings, and the source evidence frames CUST-acme-1356 as a discussion item rather than an immediate accounting action.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for the CFO-direction condition and the no-current-cycle allowance posting conclusion for CUST-acme-1356.

title: The Agent states in the support note that AR-aging bucket cleanup is formally covered by Acme Cloud Engagement Letter Addendum #1, but the May 2026 follow-through is not cleanly evidenced as a separate operational tracker in the workflow/issue/close evidence and risks being buried in the base monthly close.
justification: This is the discriminating scope-tracking nuance. A run that says the work is already cleanly tracked as post-addendum work misses the formal-versus-operational distinction.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for both sides of the conclusion: formal Addendum #1 coverage and the lack of clean separate May operational tracking.

title: The Agent states in the support note that no write-off, allowance posting, or customer-status change was made for the Acme AR-aging cleanup or the 60+ customers.
justification: The user explicitly limits the task to a collectibility read and sign-off support, not accounting or customer-account changes.
evidence: Inspect the support note content for the no-write-off, no-allowance-posting, and no-customer-status-change statement.
